sections.paragraph:
ἐκρίπτω impf. ἐξερρίπτουν 2 Macc 10:30; fut. 3 sg. ἐκρίψει Sir 10:9; 1 aor. ἐξέριψα LXX. Pass.: 2 fut. 3 sg. ἐξεριφήσεται Zeph 2:4; 2 aor. ἐξερίφην; pf. ptc. ἐξερριμμένος LXX (s. ῥίπτω; Trag. et al.; UPZ 151, 12 [259 b.c.]; PLond I, 106, 13 p. 61 [II b.c.]; LXX; TestSol 16:2 P).

① to remove with force from an area, drive away of chaff blown by the wind B 11:7 (Ps 1:4).

② to cause to be deprived of, to drive out fr., fig. ext. of 1 ἐκριφῆναι ἐκ τῆς ἐλπίδος be driven out fr. the hope = be deprived of the hope 1 Cl 57:2 (cp. Pr 5:23).

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. fr. = from

ext. ext. = extension

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 309
